questions.question: What is motion?
questions.correct_answer: Motion is the change in position of an object with respect to time.
questions.explanation: An object is said to be in motion if its position changes with time relative to a reference point.

questions.question: What is distance?
questions.correct_answer: Distance is the total path length travelled by an object.
questions.explanation: Distance is a scalar quantity and does not depend on direction.

questions.question: What is displacement?
questions.correct_answer: Displacement is the shortest distance between the initial and final positions of an object.
questions.explanation: Displacement is a vector quantity because it has both magnitude and direction.

questions.question: What is uniform motion?
questions.correct_answer: Uniform motion is when an object travels equal distances in equal intervals of time.
questions.explanation: In uniform motion the speed of the object remains constant.

questions.question: What is non-uniform motion?
questions.correct_answer: Non-uniform motion occurs when an object travels unequal distances in equal intervals of time.
questions.explanation: In this type of motion the speed or velocity of the object changes.

questions.question: What is speed?
questions.correct_answer: Speed is the distance travelled per unit time.
questions.explanation: It indicates how fast an object is moving and its SI unit is metre per second (m/s).

questions.question: What is velocity?
questions.correct_answer: Velocity is the speed of an object in a specific direction.
questions.explanation: Velocity is a vector quantity because it includes both magnitude and direction.

questions.question: What is acceleration?
questions.correct_answer: Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity with time.
questions.explanation: Acceleration occurs when an object's speed or direction changes.

questions.question: What does the slope of a distance-time graph represent?
questions.explanation: The slope of a distance-time graph shows how quickly distance changes with time.

questions.question: What are the three equations of motion?
questions.correct_answer: v = u + at, s = ut + 1/2 at², and v² − u² = 2as
questions.explanation: These equations describe motion with uniform acceleration.

notes.title: Motion Definition
notes.content: Motion refers to the change in position of an object with respect to time and a reference point.

notes.title: Distance vs Displacement
notes.content: Distance is the total path travelled by an object, whereas displacement is the shortest distance between initial and final positions.

notes.title: Speed and Velocity
notes.content: Speed is the rate of distance travelled while velocity is the rate of displacement with direction.

notes.title: Acceleration
notes.content: Acceleration describes how quickly the velocity of an object changes over time.

notes.title: Equations of Motion
notes.content: The three equations of motion relate velocity, acceleration, time, and distance for uniformly accelerated motion.

facts.fact: Distance is always greater than or equal to displacement.
The SI unit of speed and velocity is metre per second (m/s).
Acceleration can be positive or negative depending on whether velocity increases or decreases.
An object moving in a circular path with constant speed is said to have uniform circular motion.
